mentions.note: Soared alongside Intel as investors rotate into AI hardware winners.

Cramer likes AMD over Micron as potential pullback entry; beneficiary of memory chip shortage demand; better entry opportunity on any dip.
Rallied nicely as they DID factor shortage into guidance; wish we hadn't sold for Charitable Trust. Beneficiary of Intel's execution stumble.
Rallied nicely as Intel stumbled; better factored CPU shortage into guidance. Regret not holding for Trust.
Pattern has emerged where terrific numbers still result in stock decline; semiconductor sector headwind despite fundamental strength.
Potential government gating of best-in-class chip exports would cap growth and hand market to China; needs policy clarity.
Hardware and semiconductor rally; AMD killing it amid hardware-vs-software dominance shift.
Second-layer chip provider; CPU shortage emerging as AI bottleneck; up 42% since March 30; strong earnings power in genetic AI space.
CPUs for agentic AI and data center applications; alternative to Nvidia; everyone keeps buying; Cramer should have owned both AMD and Intel.
Key CPU complex player alongside Intel; should benefit from data center capex trend; up 70% since March 30.
Part of data center boom; Cramer wishes trust owned more; beneficiary of compute shortage.
Cramer pressing bet ahead of quarter; CEO Lisa Su 'is the best.' Expects meaningful upside surprise from data-center momentum and AI chip strength.
CPU shortage (agents need massive compute) mirroring storage situation; analysts calling peak premature—if shortage persists, estimates too low and stock goes higher. Use downgrades to buy.
Fantastic quarter, beautiful results; competing chip supplier with severe supply constraints; CEO Lisa Su appearing next morning.
+66 pts today; CEO Lisa Su confirmed CPU market growth 35% (vs. 18% forecast Nov); beat estimates, ignore pre-earnings downgrade FUD; valuations update overnight when facts change.
Chip/semi layer of AI cake; competing in AI semiconductor infrastructure.
Semiconductor play powering AI revolution; part of hardware cohort displacing software; benefits from data center and AI compute demand.
Alongside Intel, facing pressure from Nvidia's ARM-based CPU strategy for AI computing; x86 exposure at risk.
CPU and GPU essential for data center; CEO Lisa Sue leadership; recommended watching her MIT commencement; supplier winning from Mag 7 rotation.
